title: athlete — field classification
description: Athlete carries FOUR tiers of sensitivity: public identifiers (id, tenant_id, branch_id, first_name for internal display), internal operational data (dates, gender, dominant_hand, height/weight, status, age-group snapshot), CONFIDENTIAL (emergency contact cluster + registration_notes, gated by athlete.view.emergency_contact), REGULATED_HEALTH (medical cluster, gated by athlete.view.medical), plus REGULATED_MINOR when the athlete is a minor (age_years < config.athlete.minor_min_age). Consent flags themselves are internal-tier (the fact of a boolean isn't sensitive; the DATA those consents unlock is). The redactor pipeline applies field-level gates on egress — a caller with `athlete.view` but WITHOUT `athlete.view.medical` sees an Athlete object with medical_* keys entirely stripped from the JSON.

internal: Athlete.user_id (when linked; internal to tenant staff)
Athlete.first_name (surface value for tenant-internal display; wire-level PII when the athlete is a minor)
Athlete.date_of_birth (age-group critical; not shown to non-HR peers)
confidential: Athlete.registration_notes (HR-visible only — never surfaced to guardians)
Athlete.paused_reason (HR-visible — the reason a child was paused is confidential)
Athlete.withdrawal_reason (HR + admin visible — reason for withdrawal is compliance-relevant, not for peers)
restricted: Athlete.paused_reason (when paused_reason contains safeguarding-critical language — an HR-only sub-tier applies here; feature deferred to Wave 4+ Safeguarding module which introduces the safeguarding_officer role)
regulated_minor: For every Athlete where age_years < config.athlete.minor_min_age (default 16): the ENTIRE row inherits the minor-tier egress redaction rules. First name + last name are stripped from cross-tenant platform-admin reads (support staff never see minors' names outside the tenant plane). Photos never appear on public-facing surfaces regardless of consent_photo_release when minor. Communications route strictly through the guardian's channels — direct-to-athlete channels refused.
regulated_health: athlete_medical_history rows (dedicated audit table with 7-year retention; every mutation to a medical_* field writes here)
audit_log: Athlete.status (every transition — pause / graduate / withdraw / archive / restore)
Athlete.user_id (every link — one-way from null to non-null)
Athlete.date_of_birth (every super_admin override — should almost never happen)
Athlete.branch_id (every change — Wave 3b immutable; when the transfer flow lands, every change is audit-critical)
Athlete.medical_conditions / medical_allergies / medical_medications / medical_notes (every mutation → athlete_medical_history table with 7-year retention; NOT the normal audits table)
Athlete.emergency_contact_name / emergency_contact_phone / emergency_contact_relationship (every mutation → normal audits table, 7-year retention for confidential-tier fields)
Athlete.current_age_group_id (every snapshot flip — birthday drift + AgeGroup catalog changes)

notes: The redactor pipeline (from telemetry::RedactorProcessor) applies to every Athlete row on egress. Callers with `athlete.view.medical` see medical_*; callers with `athlete.view.medical.own` see their OWN medical fields (rare — an adult athlete viewing their own record). Callers with neither see the fields stripped from the JSON entirely.
Same pattern for emergency_contact — gated by `athlete.view.emergency_contact` (or `.own` — an adult athlete may view their own emergency contact).

Athlete.registration_notes and Athlete.paused_reason are HR-visible internal fields — they do NOT surface on the tenant activity feed. When paused_reason contains safeguarding language, it inherits the restricted tier of the enclosing context.

Medical writes DO NOT feed the activity feed. Coaches should not accidentally see 'X updated Y's allergies' scrolling past — the AthleteMedicalUpdated event routes to the medical-audit queue exclusively + the activity-feed listener DOES NOT subscribe.
The 90-day post-archive redaction on emergency_contact + medical fields aligns with GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) minimisation. Employment metadata retains 7 years for legal record.

Regulated_minor sub-tier: for minors, first_name + last_name are still shown on the tenant plane (staff MUST identify children in their care) but are STRIPPED from cross-tenant platform-admin reads. Aligns with best-practice minor-data-protection design (COPPA in the US, GDPR Art. 8 in EU).
Regulated_health rows in athlete_medical_history retain 7 years even after the parent Athlete row is hard-deleted. The retention pipeline keeps the audit rows alive per health-record regulation.
